anti_claim: Standards meta diagnostics summarizes public standards, organ, runtime, receipt coverage, and copied non-secret macro meta-diagnostics body imports only; it does not become registry authority, expose private sources, authorize release, or prove whole-system correctness.

public_private_boundary.public_inputs: public standards inventory, runtime contracts, diagnostic policy, registry refs, receipt refs, and copied non-secret macro source-module bodies with hashes and anchors

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A standards coverage board that points at registry rows and acceptance receipts while missing runtime contracts, negative-case rejection, public/private boundaries, or source-open validator evidence.
failure_modes: accepted organ admitted without a standards inventory row
accepted organ inventory row lacks standard_id, standard_ref, or registry_row_ref
accepted organ inventory row lacks current receipt refs
accepted organ lacks a runtime contract with CLI command and runtime step
standards coverage projection treated as standards registry authority
release, publication, provider-call, private-data equivalence, trading or financial advice, or whole-system correctness overclaim accepted
private source body, raw seed body, provider payload body, secret value, account/session state, browser/HUD live access, or credential-equivalent material exported through public diagnostics
secret-exclusion scan omitted or body material embedded in diagnostics receipts
synthetic receipt stand-in accepted instead of real runtime receipt refs
diagnostics bundle accepted without fixture, paper-module, standards inventory, runtime contract, and acceptance-receipt source refs
coverage map pass claimed as release readiness, product maturity, source mutation authorization, or whole-system correctness
receipt duplicates macro body text instead of carrying public refs, hashes, verdicts, and omission boundaries
exported diagnostics bundle lacks copied macro source modules and relies on pointer-shaped evidence
copied source module digest or declared anchor diverges from the target body
receipt embeds macro body text instead of carrying refs, hashes, counts, and verdicts
standard_payload.ontology: A public standards-meta diagnostics organ that checks accepted adapter-backed organs against standard mappings, runtime contracts, receipt refs, diagnostic policy ceilings, negative cases, secret-exclusion results, and copied non-secret macro body source modules.
standard_payload.teleology: Keep the Microcosm runtime spine coherent for cold readers by making standards coverage executable and by importing the real macro meta-diagnostics mechanism body without turning coverage projection into registry authority, source mutation authority, release authority, or whole-system correctness.

validation_rules: Every accepted adapter-backed organ must have a standards inventory row.
Every inventory row must carry standard_id, standard_ref, registry_row_ref, and at least one receipt ref.
Every accepted organ must have a runtime contract row with a CLI command and runtime step.
Private source bodies, provider payload bodies, release authority, provider-call authority, private-data equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims are rejected.
Exported runtime bundle must include source_module_manifest.json and copied non-secret macro body modules whose target hashes and anchors validate while keeping receipt bodies empty.
